headlines.title: Sources: Chinese startup Moonshot AI is seeking formal approval from investors to start a Hong Kong IPO process, which could happen in as soon as six months (Juro Osawa/The Information)
A British AI lab signed up Nvidia, Meta and Samsung to invent materials that don’t exist yet
Sources: parts of the Trump administration are reigniting efforts to implement de facto bans on foreign open-source models, as Chinese AI models gain momentum (Maria Curi/Axios)
An interview with Expedia CEO Ariane Gorin, who calls AI "a growth opportunity", as she prepares the online travel company to face potential disruption from AI (Stephanie Stacey/Financial Times)
How PayPal CEO Enrique Lores, appointed earlier in 2026 to revive the payments pioneer, may handle Stripe and Advent's takeover offer, based on his HP tenure (Wall Street Journal)
The firm that wires AI data centres is chasing Hong Kong’s biggest IPO since Alibaba.
Chinese open models like Kimi K3 and Qwen3.8 Max shift the industry's focus back to marginal costs and cost of goods sold, as inference costs scale with revenue (Ben Thompson/Stratechery)
Alibaba says Qwen3.8 is the world’s No.2 AI model. It has shown no proof.
15 anti-fraud startups to watch in 2026, according to investors
Etzioni on AI: Uncle Sam wants a stake in leading AI companies — what could possibly go wrong?
StrictlyVC returns to New York City September 10 to celebrate a huge year for the city’s startup community
The ‘synthetic insider’: how AI deepfakes turned the fake employee into a corporate threat
Decart’s Lucy 2.5 unlocks new live video effects, but physical AI is the real winner
Beyond grep: The case for a context-rich AI coding harness
AliExpress fined almost $630 million over illegal product sales
Mexico Extends Its Venture Lead Over Brazil As More Global VCs Enter Latin America
CuspAI lands $450m round to accelerate AI materials discovery
Prysmian signs €5.5bn fibre deal with Molex to wire AI data centres
China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
A look at Israel's influence campaign in the US that's included millions of AI-driven texts and a $45M+ contract with ex-Trump campaign strategist Brad Parscale (Wall Street Journal)
India's first privately-developed rocket reaches orbit on dramatic debut launch
This unpronounceable series of glyphs is an incredible side project from Kieran Hebden (aka Four Tet)
AI weapons under scrutiny as activists plan weekend protest at Anduril’s Seattle office
Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
